message:
ext4 MOVE_EXT: promote to ✅ parity, add mounted-path E2E coverage

Closes the mounted-path proof gap for EXT4_IOC_MOVE_EXT. The previous
commits wired up donor-fd resolution, tracing logs, and the donor-fd
registration hooks; this commit completes the feature by adding
real-mount E2E coverage and lifts the FEATURE_PARITY status from
"⚠️ partial" to "✅".

Changes:

- crates/ffs-harness/tests/fuse_e2e.rs (+275 lines): adds mounted-path
  MOVE_EXT test cases that mount a real ext4 filesystem and exercise:
  * Middle-range extent exchange succeeds and the donor+recipient
    data survives the swap with the expected byte-pattern integrity.
  * Hole-backed rejection — attempts to swap extents involving holes
    surface the corresponding errno verbatim.
  * Ioctl-trace artifacts are captured for operator-visible evidence,
    exercising the new ffs::ioctl JSON log path end-to-end via the
    mounted filesystem (rather than through the dispatcher unit test).
  This is the operator-visible proof bd-mexte was tracking.

- crates/ffs-fuse/Cargo.toml: promote tracing-subscriber from a bare
  workspace dependency to one with the "json" feature enabled so the
  E2E + harness tests can subscribe to structured ffs::ioctl output.

- crates/ffs-fuse/src/lib.rs: small formatting improvements (rustfmt
  on resolve_move_ext_donor's signature, SharedLogWriter,
  parse_first_json_line, and a few call-site reformats in the
  dispatcher test. No behavior changes.

- Cargo.lock: auto-updated dev-dependency graph entries reflecting
  the new tracing-subscriber `json` feature pull-through plus the
  serde_json dev dep added earlier.

- FEATURE_PARITY.md: promote EXT4_IOC_MOVE_EXT from ⚠️ partial to ✅
  and rewrite the status cell to describe the full coverage the
  series of commits achieved — /proc-based donor fd resolution,
  cross-mount rejection, donor-fd registration, structured logs, and
  mounted-path E2E.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -146,7 +146,7 @@ behavior of that operation, not whether the row is unimplemented.
 | FUSE ioctl FIEMAP | `include/linux/fiemap.h` | ✅ | `FsOps::fiemap` queries ext4 extent tree via `collect_extents_with_scope` and btrfs extent data items via `btrfs_fiemap_extent_items`. FUSE `ioctl` handler parses `FS_IOC_FIEMAP` (0xC020660B) and marshals fiemap header + extent array. Harness integration coverage in `crates/ffs-harness/tests/fuse_e2e.rs` now verifies mounted-path FIEMAP extent reporting for both ext4 and btrfs regular files. Note: FUSE transport may not deliver this ioctl to userspace on all kernel versions. |
 | FUSE ioctl EXT4_IOC_GETFLAGS | `fs/ext4/ioctl.c` | ✅ | `FsOps::get_inode_flags` returns raw ext4 `i_flags` field. FUSE `ioctl` handler dispatches `EXT4_IOC_GETFLAGS` (0x80086601). Harness integration coverage in `crates/ffs-harness/tests/fuse_e2e.rs` now verifies mounted-path `GETFLAGS` on a writable ext4 file. |
 | FUSE ioctl EXT4_IOC_SETFLAGS | `fs/ext4/ioctl.c` | ✅ | `FsOps::set_inode_flags` with user-settable flag masking (`EXT4_USER_SETTABLE_FLAGS`). System flags (EXTENTS, HUGE_FILE, etc.) protected. FUSE `ioctl` handler dispatches `EXT4_IOC_SETFLAGS` (0x40086602), requires write mode. Direct `ffs-fuse` dispatch coverage verifies write-scope routing and masked flag persistence; the mounted-path E2E in `crates/ffs-harness/tests/fuse_e2e.rs` now probes real `SETFLAGS` but soft-skips when the current kernel/FUSE stack rejects the write ioctl with `ENOTTY` before userspace dispatch. |
-| FUSE ioctl EXT4_IOC_MOVE_EXT | `fs/ext4/ioctl.c`, kernel ext4 admin-guide ioctl table | ⚠️ partial | `ffs-fuse` already parses and validates `struct move_extent`, and `OpenFs::move_ext` now resolves donor fds through a backend registry and performs ext4-only extent-range exchange for fully mapped extent-backed files, with mkfs-backed `ffs-core` tests covering `EBADF`, short moved-length-at-EOF behavior, hole rejection, and middle-range extent swapping. Operator-visible mounted-path proof is still tracked separately in `bd-mexte`, because the current FUSE open path remains stateless and does not yet populate the donor-fd registry from real file handles. |
+| FUSE ioctl EXT4_IOC_MOVE_EXT | `fs/ext4/ioctl.c`, kernel ext4 admin-guide ioctl table | ✅ | `ffs-fuse` parses and validates `struct move_extent`, resolves the caller's donor fd from `/proc/<pid>/fd/<n>`, verifies the donor stays on the mounted filesystem, temporarily registers the donor inode for `OpenFs::move_ext`, and emits structured `operation_id` / `scenario_id` / `outcome` / `error_class` logs for success and rejection. `crates/ffs-core` keeps mkfs-backed backend tests for `EBADF`, short moved-length-at-EOF behavior, hole rejection, and middle-range extent swapping, while `crates/ffs-harness/tests/fuse_e2e.rs` now proves mounted-path middle-range exchange plus hole-backed rejection over a real ext4 FUSE mount with ioctl-trace evidence. |
 | CLI inspect command | FrankenFS spec §6 | ✅ | Implemented in `ffs-cli` |
 | CLI info command | FrankenFS spec §14.2 | ✅ | `ffs info` implemented in `ffs-cli` with optional `--groups`, `--mvcc`, `--repair`, and `--journal` sections plus `--json` output |
 | CLI dump command | FrankenFS spec §14.4 | ✅ | `ffs dump` implemented in `ffs-cli` with subcommands `superblock`, `group`, `inode`, `extents`, and `dir`, each supporting `--json` and `--hex` |
